title: acer opulifolium subsp. neapolitanum
intro: Acer opulifolium subsp. neapolitanum, also known as the Naples maple, is a subspecies of maple tree native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate digestion, treat respiratory issues, and reduce inflammation. The leaves of Acer opulifolium subsp. neapolitanum have been used to make herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, which are said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating coughs, and promoting relaxation. Additionally, the plant's bark and roots are used to make remedies for various ailments, including digestive problems and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
